task: I had a customer ask for a refund on a card charge after already leaving, and I want to handle it the right way without getting burned. What should I keep on hand or write down if I need to protect myself later?

turns.agent_response:
Keep a simple paper trail. You want enough to show what happened, without making it a big production.

Have on hand:
- The signed receipt or closed check
- The original ticket
- A note of the table number, time, and amount
- The card’s last 4 digits if available
- The name of the guest or the name they gave you
- Any manager approval if a refund is issued
- A quick note if the guest came back after leaving and what they said

What to write down:
- Date and time
- Table number
- Amount charged
- Item(s) disputed
- What the guest claimed
- What you told them
- Whether you said, “I’m verifying it before I say one way or the other”
- Whether you checked the ticket and what it showed
- Who handled it next

Example note:
“4/29, Table 12, $18.50 charge disputed after guest left. Guest said burger was wrong. Told them I’m verifying it before I say one way or the other. Checked ticket; burger ordered as printed. Sent to manager for review.”

If you want, I can also give you a super short shift-note version you can copy in 10 seconds.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah. That’s the right kind of paper trail.

I’d keep it even tighter though:
- closed check / signed receipt
- original ticket
- exact time
- table number
- amount
- last 4 on the card if you’ve got it
- what they claimed was wrong
- who you handed it to after

And write the part that matters: what was actually on the ticket, not their story. If they left and came back running their mouth, note that too. That’s the kind of thing people forget later when they start acting confused.

The example note is fine. Short, clean, factual. That’s what holds up.
